rag_rules: Fix unresolved references in AGREE contracts by ensuring all identifiers are visible or properly declared in the same annex scope.
generic_rules: When validating AGREE contracts, trace type errors to their root cause in identifier resolution or helper declarations.
Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers within the same annex scope to avoid 'named thing must be an expression with a type' errors.
Remove invalid guarantee clauses instead of adding architecture when root references are unresolved.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks with proper scoping of visible identifiers.
Component implementation annexes are used for bindings like assignments, while component type annexes hold assumptions and guarantees.
anti_patterns: Adding new architecture elements to fix AGREE diagnostics without resolving the underlying unresolved references.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about property associations not pointing to anything
